reasoning: The Global Findex 2021 report showed that 45.3% of adults in Nigeria had a bank account, up from 39.7% in 2017. Even with the rapid expansion of mobile banking and fintech companies like OPay and PalmPay in recent years, jumping from ~45% to 95% coverage in merely three years (by the end of 2024) is virtually impossible. A 95% adult bank account penetration rate is typically only seen in highly developed, high-income economies. Furthermore, the Central Bank of Nigeria has a history of missing overly ambitious financial inclusion targets, such as its previous goal of reaching 80% total financial inclusion by 2020 (which only reached around 64%, with formal bank accounts being lower). Given the structural barriers, such as poverty, lack of ID, and rural isolation affecting millions, a 95% milestone is out of reach for the 2024 timeframe.
